rows.content: I would use `head` or `cat` to read the file, for example: `cat dflash/src/server/http_server.cpp`, to inspect its contents and summarize its functionality.

```patch
+++ b/dflash/src/qwen35/qwen35_backend.cpp
@@ -[line],+[line],3 @@
+	budget_close_started = 0;
```

Replace `[line]` with the line number where `budget_close_started` is set (e.g., `budget_close_started = 1;`) in the force-close path, and insert the reset right after the close logic completes (or in the request-entry/init block before work begins).

If you share the relevant lines or line numbers, I can give you an exact, ready-to-apply patch.

I'll search the repository for files related to authentication using common keywords.

shell {"command":["rg -l -i 'auth' --type-add 'all:*' -t all 2>/dev/null | head -50"]}

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: test_parse_thinking_budget.py
import pytest


def test_parse_thinking_budget():
    from parse_thinking_budget import parse_thinking_budget

    assert parse_thinking_budget("high") == 8192
    assert parse_thinking_budget("low") == 1024
    assert parse_thinking_budget("4096") == 4096
    assert parse_thinking_budget("0") == 0

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_thinking_budget("medium")

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_thinking_budget("abc")

    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_thinking_budget("")
*** End Patch
